title: Rebuild GestureHotkeyApp after unified trigger capture implementation
bootstrap_instructions: Read ai-executor/AGENTS.md before starting work if available.
Read ai-executor/AI_ORCHESTRATOR_RUNTIME.md because this task is executed via AI Orchestrator runtime JSON if available.
Read README.md and docs/codex-handoff-spec.md before running build commands.
Use source-v1.0.1/7-rate-OpenMouseGesture-b8f5357 as the active source directory.
Do not infer unreadable files. Record unreadable files in runtime/report.json.
purpose: Build the latest pushed main branch containing Unified Trigger Capture so the user can test the new Trigger A/B/C input capture behavior with a fresh GestureHotkeyApp.exe.
background: Unified Trigger Capture was implemented and pushed to origin/main as commit b695482 feat: implement unified mouse and keyboard trigger capture. The existing local GestureHotkeyApp.exe may be from before that implementation. The immediate need is not additional feature work, but to pull the latest source, rebuild, and report the exact fresh executable and installer paths, sizes, and timestamps for user testing.
scope: In scope: safe git sync, verify latest commit includes b695482 or successor containing unified trigger capture, run build commands, regenerate exe and installer, report paths. Out of scope: new feature implementation, broad refactoring, installer execution, release upload, action label overlay re-enablement, and manual GUI testing beyond optional launch/smoke checks if already safe.
constraints: Do not perform destructive git operations such as reset --hard, clean -fd, force push, branch deletion, or unreviewed mass deletion.
Do not run the installer unless the human explicitly permits it.
Do not upload artifacts to GitHub Releases or external services.
Do not commit generated target/ outputs unless explicitly instructed later.
Do not re-enable action label overlay.
Do not make additional feature changes unless the build is blocked by a minimal fix clearly required for compilation.
If the working tree is dirty before pull, stop and report exact files rather than discarding changes.
required_changes: Pull or fast-forward to origin/main safely.
Confirm the local history includes b695482 or a later commit containing feat: implement unified mouse and keyboard trigger capture.
Run npm run tauri build or the existing build helper if needed for Visual Studio Build Tools environment.
Verify fresh GestureHotkeyApp.exe exists and record full path, size, and LastWriteTime.
Verify fresh NSIS installer exists and record full path, size, and LastWriteTime.
Report whether GestureHotkeyApp-setup.exe exists or not.
Do not perform manual GUI validation unless it is safe and does not require installer execution. If GUI validation is not done, mark it unverified.
validation: git status --short is checked before and after build.
npm run build result is recorded.
npm run tauri build or build helper result is recorded.
Fresh generated executable path, size, and timestamp are recorded.
Fresh generated installer path, size, and timestamp are recorded.
runtime/report.json is updated with success, failed, or blocked status.
completion_criteria: A fresh build using the latest unified trigger capture source has completed successfully, or a blocker is clearly identified.
The user can identify which GestureHotkeyApp.exe to run for testing.
Generated artifact paths, sizes, and timestamps are reported.
No destructive git operation, installer execution, release upload, or unrelated source change was performed.
reporting_requirements: Write runtime/report.json with task_id, status, summary, git status, latest commit checked, commands run, build results, generated exe path/size/timestamp, generated installer path/size/timestamp, files changed, unverified items, blockers, and next recommended user test.
notes: The user is waiting to test the new trigger capture behavior. The most important output is the exact path to the freshly rebuilt GestureHotkeyApp.exe.
